codeReview.summary: The worker did not produce or identify a feature-owned implementation commit/diff. The handoff explicitly states the feature remained partial because the focused regression matrix was already present from earlier commits, so there is no concrete feature implementation to review against VAL-TEST-001.

issues.description: The feature has no committed implementation artifact: the handoff marks successState as partial, reports that no isolatable diff existed for this feature, and therefore provides no commitId to inspect. Without a feature-owned diff, scrutiny cannot verify that this feature itself established the focused regression matrix promised by VAL-TEST-001.

features.json still marks milestone-f-focused-regression-matrix-separation as completed even though the worker handoff says the work was only partially handled and required orchestrator follow-up. The mission state does not point scrutiny to a concrete commit or proof artifact for this feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker procedure assumes a feature can always be driven by adding failing tests first and producing a feature-scoped commit, but this cleanup feature was effectively a validation-mapping/review task after sibling milestone-f commits had already landed the underlying proofs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The transcript for worker session 22a05869-819a-4dcd-8c12-7f006fbcac32 ends in a partial handoff stating no feature-owned diff existed, while the skill still instructs workers in steps 6-8 and 37-41 to create failing tests, implement, isolate a diff, and commit it.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission state does not document how scrutiny should map proof-separation features onto already-landed sibling commits when no dedicated commit exists. This leaves validators and reviewers unable to tie VAL-TEST-001 to a concrete artifact even though the repo contains the relevant focused regressions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The handoff cites existing tests such as create_at_duplicate_id_returns_typed_conflict, schema_managed_runtime_applies_registered_inventory, decode_raw_row_helper_accepts_string_and_object_record_links, and successful_foreign_save_matches_all_read_entrypoints, but no mission artifact records that these proofs are intentionally satisfied by commits 728f3d5/3ff4d10 or another concrete source.

summary: Fail: no feature-specific commit or diff was available to review, so the focused-regression-matrix-separation feature lacks auditable implementation evidence even though related focused regressions exist elsewhere in the repo.
